revision.text:
[[ja:天宮神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Amemiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Morimachi Amenomiya-jinja ac (2).jpg |location={{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shūchi District, Shizuoka|en|Shūchi District, Shizuoka|qid=Q1205501|lt=Shuchi District|1=Shūchi District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Morimachi (Shizuoka)|ja|森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimachi (Shizuoka)|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q854613}} Amemiya 576 |LatitudeDegrees=34 |LatitudeMinutes=50 |LatitudeSeconds=38.6 |NS=N |LongitudeDegrees=137 |LongitudeMinutes=56 |LongitudeSeconds=03.6 |EW=E |deity={{ill|Takiiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} |Rank=Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Established=Era of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} |MainHall= |Alias= |TempleAffiliation= |AnnualFestival=Grand Festival (First Saturday and Sunday of April) |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Mori|qid=Q1345571|1=Mori, Shizuoka}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.844055555555556|N|137.93433333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Amemiya Shrine''' (天宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Morimachi (Shizuoka)|ja|森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimachi (Shizuoka)|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q854613}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine is dedicated to the {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, collectively known as ''Michinushinomochi'' (道主貴): * {{ill|Takiribime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Tagorihime no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) == History == [[File:Morimachi Amenomiya-jinja ac (3).jpg|thumb|Dance Pavilion]] According to shrine tradition, during the reign of the 29th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, descendants of {{ill|Ousu-no-Mikoto|ja|小碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ousu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}, specifically the Ōta and Mori clans, searched for ancestral relics and settled in this area. They welcomed the spirit of the Munakata Great deity from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|qid=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}} and enshrined it here. The tradition of the {{ill|Twelve-stage Bugaku|ja|天宮神社の十二段舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelve-stage Bugaku|qq=}} dance dates back to 705 CE (Keiun 2), when court official Fujiwara Ayatari, posted to the region, introduced Kyoto’s court dances as an offering.[https://www.shiz-bunka.com/pages/child_detail019 天宮神社例大祭 - Fujinokuni Cultural Resource Database], accessed March 23, 2018. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, Nakamura Noritaka, the 66th head priest, compiled tales of local strange phenomena in a collection titled ''Jijitsu Shōdan''.Konno Mizuki, "Tales of Spirit Possession by Shinto Priests," in Ooyama Satoko, ed., ''Healing Illness and Magic in Pre-Modern Japan'' (Shibunkaku Publishing, 2020), pp. 256–279. The dance is currently designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan under the name ''Tōtōmi Morimachi Bugaku.''[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/199756 Cultural Heritage Online], accessed May 26, 2018. == Grounds == [[File:Morimachi Amenomiya-jinja ac (4).jpg|thumb|180px|The sacred Nagi tree of Amemiya Shrine]] To the east of the main hall stands a sacred {{ill|Nageia nagi|en|Nageia nagi|qid=Q3274110|lt=Nagi tree|1=Nageia nagi}}, estimated to be over 1,000 years old, with a height of 18 meters and a canopy spread of 9 meters.[https://www.chuen.net/odekake/meguri_2020_02.html 天宮神社のナギ], Chuen Regional Association. On January 30, 1954, it was designated a Natural Monument of Shizuoka Prefecture under the name ''Amemiya Shrine's Nagi.''[https://www.town.morimachi.shizuoka.jp/gyosei/machinososhiki/shakaikyoikuka/bunkashinkogakari/1/1096.html Cultural Properties], Morimachi. The tree is believed to have been brought from the shrine’s parent, {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|qid=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}}.[https://www.town.morimachi.shizuoka.jp/kanko/jinja/amenomiya.html Amemiya Shrine], Morimachi, accessed March 23, 2018. == location == '''Address''' * Amemiya 576, {{ill|Morimachi (Shizuoka)|ja|森町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimachi (Shizuoka)|1=Morimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q854613}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Nearest station: Disembark at {{ill|Tenryū Hamanako Line|en|Tenryū Hamanako Line|qid=Q5361916|lt=Tenryū Hamanako Line|1=Tenryū Hamanako Line}} {{ill|Towata Station|en|Towata Station|qid=Q5369980|lt=Towata Station|1=Towata Station}}, approximately a 10-minute walk. * Bus: Take the Akiha Bus Service or Morimachi Community Bus Yoshikawa Line to "Morinaka Entrance" stop, a 6-minute walk from there. From {{ill|Fukuroi Station|en|Fukuroi Station|qid=Q871711|lt=Fukuroi Station|1=Fukuroi Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Line (Shizuoka area)|en|Tōkaidō Line (Shizuoka area)|qid=Q11527981|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Shizuoka)|1=Tōkaidō Line (Shizuoka area)}}, the ride takes approximately 40 minutes. '''Nearby Sites''' * {{ill|Okuni Shrine|en|Okuni Shrine|qid=Q421597|lt=Oguni Shrine|1=Okuni Shrine}} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|1=Munakata Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451876}} * {{ill|Twelve-stage Bugaku|ja|天宮神社の十二段舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelve-stage Bugaku|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://morimachiamenomiyajinjya.jimdo.com/ Official Website] * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4410002 Amemiya Shrine] (Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office) {{Munakata Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|天宮神社|version=104616999|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天宮神社]] {{wikidata link|Q30934659}} [[Category:Munakata shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:静岡県森町の建築物]] [[Category:静岡県森町の歴史]]
